title: Make picture graphs
description: Practice creating picture graphs (pictographs) from data sets.

assessment_items.question_content:
Some students collected ladybugs.

Student | Number of bugs
:- | :-: | -
Jen | $4$ 
Lee | $6$ 
Sara | $5$ 
 
**Create a picture graph of this data.**

 ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6283806fe5bb605719bfaddad9753270a064e9ec)

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints: Each ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/35ebf3ab9bfcb72b5774e2c0374e73870fd52833) equals $\blueE1$ ladybug.

Jen had $\maroonD{4}$ ladybugs, so we put $\maroonD4$ pictures above Jen.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/397c0664d707b0a4def5bdcab5300bbc6b1ce5af)
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $2$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f5709dda308564f88f84013382adec0317fef54f)

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints: Each ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9753371c3f82ff765eb49f6a27883b7ba3259fe4) equals $\blueE1$ egg.

Omar had $\maroonD{6}$ eggs, so we put $\maroonD6$ pictures above Omar.

Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $3$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:

[[☃ plotter 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Buddy and his dog friends love to hide bones! The picture graph shows how many bones each of them hid this week.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/013f9443299cfa8d11bb59fb64f9df7800501e45)  
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27ffdf8a282fda004e1d5b250a6229c11a3bdb6e)

**Complete the chart using the picture graph to show how many bones each dog hid.**

Dog | Number of bones hidden
- | :-: | -
Spot| [[☃ input-number 1]]
Buddy | [[☃ input-number 2]]
Snickers| [[☃ input-number 3]]
Socks| [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints: Each ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2f834b165b2efbf94d3d9c2b834874f.png) equals $\blueE{1}$ bone.

We need to count how many pictures are over each dog on the picture graph.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82349f975db382695772f9b1c7f3b6cb44e5b686)

There are $\blueE4$ pictures over Spot, so he hid $4$ bones.

Now, let's count the pictures for the other columns.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9478a5b0d3532580ccb1860982eb4499d425bf0b)

Here is the completed chart:

Dog |  Number of bones hidden
- | :-: | :-:
Spot| $\blueE{4}$
Buddy| $\purpleD6$
Snickers| $\goldE3$
Socks| $\tealD8$

assessment_items.question_content:
An ice cream shop counted how many strawberry ice cream cones they sold for five days.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ebcacce18c1787091a0689b08b9b7cc5fdd797ea)  
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cdbcaf2f0cb5dbe9ad9e7fa077dced2823040ed9)

**Complete the chart using the picture graph to show how many ice cream cones were sold each day of the week.**

Day of the week | Number of cones sold
- | :-: | -
Monday| [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Tuesday| [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Wednesday | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
Thursday| [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
Friday| [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints: Each ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1a7d0ad4b6a3426734f10d116b8e1b12d25f60c) equals $\blueE{1}$ ice cream cone.

We need to count how many pictures are above each day of the week on the picture graph.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3984c098e1b0881c0009a1946cca2fbad16d9fa1)

There are $\blueE2$ pictures above Monday so they sold $2$ ice cream cones on Monday.

Now, let's count the pictures for the other columns.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/47917fb0f14f8aee74326c96c6d6e8ae99816e0e)

Here is the completed chart:

Day of the week |  Number of cones sold
- | :-: | :-:
Monday | $\blueE{2}$
Tuesday | $\purpleE3$
Wednesday | $\goldE6$
Thursday | $\tealD3$
Friday | $\maroonD7$
